user_message: Help me prep the forecast prep for Contoso Ltd. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-01 09:31 EST
Subject: Re: Re: forecast prep Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for forecast prep. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-11 15:20 EST
Subject: forecast prep - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with forecast prep. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 15:09 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Contoso Ltd forecast prep

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with forecast prep. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Contoso Ltd
Date: 2024-02-13
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Priya (Contoso Ltd)

[00:02:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on forecast prep from our last session.

[00:05:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:08:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:12:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:16:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:18:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:27:00] Priya (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:29:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): On the forecast prep front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-K failure mode (One-page constraint hurts complex meetings)

Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence
